refined_fr.refined_fr: Enable measurement and reporting of material throughput in volumetric or mass units per unit time.
refined_fr.rationale: The original FR conflates two distinct obligations: (1) the system's capability to measure/report throughput metrics, and (2) the achievement of a customer-defined target. The latter is a success criterion dependent on operational execution, not a system requirement. The refined FR isolates the system's functional obligation—providing visibility into throughput—which is solution-neutral and testable independent of whether a specific productivity target is met. The target itself belongs in acceptance criteria or KPI definitions, not in the FR.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Demonstrate that the NGHE solution captures and reports material throughput (m³/h or ton/h) during representative operations. Verify data accuracy against independent measurement. Confirm reporting is accessible to Site Manager and relevant stakeholders in real time or near-real time.
sub_frs: FR-NGHE-001.1: Acquire material throughput data (volume or mass per time interval) during active operations.
FR-NGHE-001.2: Calculate and aggregate throughput metrics from acquired data.
FR-NGHE-001.3: Communicate throughput metrics to authorized stakeholders in a human-interpretable format.

candidate_dps: DP1: Acquire instantaneous flow rate via continuous sensing of material stream properties (mass or volume per unit time) and integrate over evaluation period to compute cumulative throughput
DP2: Measure discrete material batches (count, mass, or volume per batch) at regular intervals and extrapolate to throughput rate over evaluation period
DP3: Monitor material level change in a bounded reservoir (input or output) over time and derive throughput from level-delta and cross-sectional geometry
DP4: Combine multiple independent measurement modalities (e.g., batch counting + continuous sensing) with reconciliation logic to report throughput with confidence bounds
DP5: Instrument material transport mechanism (conveyor speed, pump displacement, bucket fill cycles) and compute throughput from mechanism kinematics and material properties
selected_dp.selected_dp: DP1: Acquire instantaneous flow rate via continuous sensing of material stream properties (mass or volume per unit time) and integrate over evaluation period to compute cumulative throughput

potential_sources: Sensor selection (mass vs. volumetric) is decoupled from flow-rate acquisition logic; choice depends on material type and acceptance criteria, not on measurement principle
Integration method (numerical summation, time-weighted averaging) is independent of sensor modality
Reporting interface (data format, frequency, units) can be decoupled from acquisition via a standardized data contract
Calibration and drift compensation are localized to sensor module; reporting logic remains agnostic
coupling_check.notes: DP1 exhibits low coupling because the core concept—continuous instantaneous measurement + temporal integration—is orthogonal to sensor technology, material properties, and reporting format. Sensor selection and signal conditioning are implementation concerns (IM layer), not design-level dependencies. Risk: if material properties vary significantly (density, particle size), volumetric vs. mass sensing choice may require re-evaluation, but this is a constraint-driven refinement, not a design flaw.

assumptions_risks: Assumption: Material flow is continuous or quasi-continuous during evaluation period; risk: if flow is highly intermittent or bursty, instantaneous sensing may require high temporal resolution and buffering, increasing cost and complexity.

Assumption: Sensor accuracy and repeatability are sufficient to meet productivity_target acceptance criterion; risk: sensor drift, calibration drift, or environmental interference (temperature, humidity, electromagnetic) could cause reported throughput to diverge from true throughput.

Assumption: Material properties (density for mass-to-volume conversion, if needed) are known or measurable; risk: if material composition varies unpredictably, conversion errors propagate into throughput error.
Assumption: Evaluation period is long enough to average out transient effects and sensor noise; risk: short evaluation windows may amplify measurement uncertainty relative to target.

Assumption: Integration method (cumulative sum, time-weighted average) is compatible with worksite operational policies and safety plan; risk: if real-time reporting is required for safety interlocks, latency in integration could be problematic.

Assumption: Sensor installation does not disrupt material flow or create safety hazards (e.g., blockage, spillage, electrical hazard); risk: intrusive sensors may require additional guarding or maintenance.
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Refine FR to explicitly state: (1) required temporal resolution of throughput reporting (e.g., instantaneous, 1-minute rolling average, end-of-period aggregate); (2) acceptable measurement uncertainty (e.g., ±5% of productivity_target); (3) whether material properties (density, particle size distribution) are stable or variable; (4) whether real-time feedback is required for operational control or safety. These constraints will inform IM-layer decisions (sensor type, signal conditioning, integration frequency) and validate DP1 feasibility. Additionally, cross-reference worksite safety plan to identify any constraints on sensor placement, power supply, or data transmission that could couple DP1 to specific implementation choices.

im_boundary.name: Vehicle
im_boundary.scope: Onboard sensing, actuation, and real-time data acquisition subsystems including flow rate measurement hardware, sensor integration, signal conditioning, and local cumulative throughput computation

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Vehicle transmits instantaneous flow rate and cumulative throughput measurements to external systems (Teleoperation, NGHE Solution) at periodic intervals

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: Vehicle receives evaluation period duration and flow measurement configuration parameters from Teleoperation or NGHE Solution

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Vehicle uses network link to transmit telemetry and receive tasking commands; may also receive real-time calibration or sensor diagnostics

module_containment_scores.notes: Strong containment: continuous sensing hardware, signal conditioning, and local integration logic are naturally vehicle-resident. Diffusion occurs only at telemetry export boundary (measurement results transmitted externally). Sensor calibration and evaluation period parameters must cross boundary via Tasking interface.

Weak containment: NGHE Solution is a high-level coordination/planning layer. Placing continuous sensing and real-time integration here creates tight coupling to vehicle hardware and introduces latency. Sensor data would need to flow from Vehicle to NGHE and back, violating locality of real-time measurement.

Weak containment: Teleoperation is a command/control interface layer, not a sensing layer. Placing flow measurement logic here decouples it from hardware, introduces network latency, and creates unnecessary dependency on operator presence. Real-time integration would suffer.

Poor containment: Safety Services is a cross-cutting concern for hazard monitoring and alert generation. Flow rate measurement is a functional capability, not a safety function. Placing it here conflates measurement with safety logic and creates diffusion across multiple safety-unrelated consumers.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: The Vehicle module is the natural owner of continuous flow rate sensing and cumulative throughput integration because: (1) sensing hardware and signal conditioning are physically resident on the vehicle; (2) real-time integration requires low-latency local computation; (3) measurement results are exported via Telemetry interface to consumers (NGHE Solution, Teleoperation, Safety Services) without requiring bidirectional coupling; (4) Tasking interface provides a clean input boundary for evaluation period and calibration parameters.
containment_risks: Sensor calibration drift over time may require remote updates from NGHE Solution, creating a secondary feedback loop via Tasking interface.
If multiple independent evaluation periods must run concurrently (e.g., short-term and long-term throughput tracking), Vehicle module must manage state for each period without becoming a bottleneck.
Network latency in Telemetry export may cause consumers to receive stale flow rate data; Vehicle must timestamp measurements and clearly indicate integration window boundaries.
If Safety Services requires real-time flow rate thresholds for hazard detection, Vehicle must export instantaneous flow rate (not just cumulative) via Telemetry to avoid safety-critical diffusion.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: High. The FR acceptance criterion (reported productivity ≥ productivity_target over evaluation period) is directly measurable via the Vehicle's Telemetry interface. Cumulative throughput computation is deterministic and auditable. Evaluation period boundaries are controlled via Tasking interface, enabling reproducible measurement windows. Worksite safety plan integration is external to this FR and does not impede measurement verification.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: Clear. The Vehicle module owns the complete measurement pipeline: hardware sensing → signal conditioning → real-time integration → cumulative result export. Consumers (NGHE Solution, Teleoperation, Safety Services) receive results via Telemetry without needing to participate in measurement logic. Tasking interface provides unambiguous input boundary for configuration parameters.

fr_dp_gate.reason: DP1 (continuous sensing + integration over evaluation period) directly and sufficiently addresses the FR (enable measurement and reporting of throughput in volumetric or mass units per unit time). The DP specifies the logical solution (instantaneous flow rate acquisition and cumulative integration) without over-constraining implementation. The approach is sound for both mass-based and volume-based measurement modes.

diffusion_risks: Sensor calibration drift feedback loop: If NGHE Solution must push calibration updates to Vehicle via Tasking interface, this creates a secondary coupling. Mitigation: define calibration update protocol with clear versioning and timestamp semantics to prevent stale calibration application.

Concurrent evaluation periods: If Vehicle must track multiple independent throughput windows simultaneously (e.g., short-term and long-term metrics), state management complexity increases. Mitigation: Vehicle module must pre-allocate state slots for concurrent periods and document maximum supported concurrency.

Instantaneous flow rate export for Safety Services: If Safety Services requires real-time flow thresholds for hazard detection, Vehicle must export both instantaneous flow rate AND cumulative throughput via Telemetry. Failure to export instantaneous data would force Safety Services to derive it from cumulative deltas, introducing latency and diffusion. Mitigation: Telemetry interface must include instantaneous flow rate as a distinct field alongside cumulative throughput.

Network latency in Telemetry: Consumers receive stale measurement data if network delays exceed the measurement reporting interval. Mitigation: Vehicle must timestamp all Telemetry messages with measurement window boundaries and indicate data freshness explicitly.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: Vehicle module achieves 0.85 containment score with justified ownership. Continuous sensing hardware, signal conditioning, and local integration logic are naturally vehicle-resident and satisfy locality-of-reference for real-time computation. Diffusion is minimal and confined to measurement result export (Telemetry) and configuration input (Tasking), both of which are clean, unidirectional interfaces. NGHE Solution (0.4 containment), Teleoperation (0.35 containment), and Safety Services (0.3 containment) are correctly identified as poor candidates due to latency sensitivity, hardware decoupling, and functional misalignment. No evidence of scattered DP responsibility across multiple modules.

recommended_revisions: Explicitly define Telemetry message schema to include: (1) instantaneous flow rate (mass/volume per unit time), (2) cumulative throughput (total mass/volume), (3) evaluation period start/end timestamps, (4) measurement window duration, (5) sensor health/calibration status. This ensures Safety Services and NGHE Solution can consume real-time flow data without secondary diffusion.

Document Tasking interface protocol for evaluation period configuration: specify maximum concurrent periods, period duration constraints, and calibration update semantics (versioning, timestamp, rollback behavior).

Add Vehicle module specification section detailing: (a) supported flow measurement modalities (mass vs. volume), (b) sensor types and accuracy specifications, (c) integration algorithm (e.g., trapezoidal, rectangular), (d) maximum reporting latency, (e) state management for concurrent evaluation periods.

Define acceptance test procedure: (1) configure evaluation period via Tasking, (2) run representative material throughput operation, (3) collect Telemetry stream, (4) verify cumulative throughput ≥ productivity_target, (5) validate timestamp consistency and measurement window boundaries.

Clarify Safety Services interaction: if real-time flow rate thresholds are required for hazard detection, confirm that Vehicle exports instantaneous flow rate via Telemetry and that Safety Services does NOT attempt to compute flow rate from cumulative deltas (which would introduce diffusion and latency).

decision.justification: All three gates (FR-IM, FR-DP, DP-IM) pass with high confidence, and the Vehicle module achieves strong containment (0.85) with clean interface boundaries. However, the DP-IM gate identifies five concrete diffusion risks that must be resolved before proceeding to sub-level decomposition. The safety criticality of this FR (high) amplifies the consequence of unmitigated diffusion: (1) stale calibration application could corrupt throughput measurement, (2) concurrent evaluation period state management could cause metric collision, (3) missing instantaneous flow rate export would force Safety Services into secondary derivation (latency + diffusion), (4) network latency could deliver stale data to safety-critical consumers, (5) Safety Services interaction model is ambiguous. These are not blockers to the FR-DP-IM logic itself, but they are architectural clarifications that must be locked in at the current level before decomposing further. Proceeding to sub-FRs without resolving these would propagate ambiguity downward and create rework. The recommended_revisions are precise and actionable; implementing them will harden the DP and IM specifications without invalidating the FR.

revise.why: The DP (continuous sensing + integration over evaluation period) is logically sound but under-specified in critical areas that directly impact safety and measurement integrity. Revisions must address: (1) Telemetry message schema must explicitly include instantaneous flow rate, cumulative throughput, evaluation period timestamps, and sensor health status—this is a DP-level specification, not an IM detail, because it defines the logical measurement output contract. (2) Tasking interface protocol for evaluation period configuration must specify maximum concurrent periods, duration constraints, and calibration update semantics (versioning, timestamp, rollback)—this is a DP-level input contract. (3) Integration algorithm selection (trapezoidal vs. rectangular) and sensor accuracy specifications must be stated at DP level to ensure the approach is sound for both mass and volume modalities. (4) Safety Services interaction model must be clarified: if real-time flow thresholds are required for hazard detection, the DP must explicitly commit to exporting instantaneous flow rate and prohibit Safety Services from deriving it from cumulative deltas. (5) Network latency and data freshness handling must be specified in the DP (e.g., timestamp all Telemetry messages with measurement window boundaries, indicate staleness explicitly). These revisions are DP-level because they define the logical solution contract; they do not change the fundamental approach but make it unambiguous and safety-aware.

assumptions: Vehicle module has access to continuous flow sensing hardware (mass or volume) with sufficient accuracy to meet productivity_target measurement tolerance.
Tasking interface can reliably deliver evaluation period configuration (start time, duration, modality) to Vehicle module with deterministic latency.

Telemetry interface can deliver measurement results (instantaneous flow rate, cumulative throughput, timestamps) to NGHE Solution, Teleoperation, and Safety Services with bounded latency acceptable for their respective use cases.
Calibration updates from NGHE Solution to Vehicle are infrequent and can be versioned/timestamped to prevent stale application.
Safety Services does not require sub-millisecond instantaneous flow rate updates; reporting interval of Vehicle Telemetry is acceptable for hazard detection thresholds.
Vehicle module can pre-allocate state for concurrent evaluation periods (e.g., short-term and long-term throughput windows) without exceeding memory or computational constraints.
Network infrastructure between Vehicle and consumers (NGHE Solution, Safety Services) provides sufficient reliability and latency bounds to support measurement-driven decision-making.
open_risks: Calibration drift feedback loop: If NGHE Solution pushes calibration updates via Tasking interface, stale or conflicting calibration versions could corrupt throughput measurement. Mitigation required: define versioning and timestamp semantics, implement rollback protocol, add calibration status to Telemetry export.

Concurrent evaluation period state collision: If Vehicle tracks multiple independent throughput windows simultaneously and state management is not pre-allocated, metric collision or overflow could occur. Mitigation required: pre-allocate state slots, document maximum concurrency, add state health indicators to Telemetry.

Instantaneous flow rate latency for Safety Services: If Safety Services requires real-time flow thresholds for hazard detection but Vehicle only exports cumulative throughput, Safety Services will derive instantaneous flow from cumulative deltas, introducing latency and diffusion. Mitigation required: Telemetry must include instantaneous flow rate as a distinct field; Safety Services must NOT compute flow from deltas.

Network latency in Telemetry delivery: Consumers may receive stale measurement data if network delays exceed reporting interval. Mitigation required: Vehicle must timestamp all Telemetry messages with measurement window boundaries and indicate data freshness explicitly.

Safety Services interaction model ambiguity: Unclear whether Safety Services requires instantaneous flow rate for hazard detection or only cumulative throughput. If instantaneous rate is required, current DP may be insufficient. Clarification required before sub-level decomposition.
evidence_needed: Telemetry message schema specification (JSON or protobuf) including: instantaneous flow rate (units, precision), cumulative throughput (units, precision), evaluation period start/end timestamps (ISO 8601 or epoch), measurement window duration, sensor health/calibration status flags.

Tasking interface protocol specification: maximum concurrent evaluation periods, period duration constraints (min/max), calibration update semantics (versioning scheme, timestamp format, rollback procedure).

Vehicle module specification document: (a) supported flow measurement modalities (mass vs. volume), (b) sensor types and accuracy specifications (e.g., ±2% of full scale), (c) integration algorithm (trapezoidal, rectangular, or other), (d) maximum reporting latency (e.g., ≤500 ms), (e) state management architecture for concurrent periods.

Safety Services requirements clarification: Does Safety Services require instantaneous flow rate for real-time hazard detection? If yes, confirm Vehicle Telemetry exports instantaneous flow rate and Safety Services does NOT derive it from cumulative deltas.

Network latency analysis: Measure or specify maximum latency between Vehicle Telemetry generation and consumer receipt. Confirm latency is acceptable for all consumers (NGHE Solution, Teleoperation, Safety Services).

Acceptance test procedure: (1) configure evaluation period via Tasking, (2) run representative material throughput operation, (3) collect Telemetry stream, (4) verify cumulative throughput ≥ productivity_target, (5) validate timestamp consistency and measurement window boundaries, (6) confirm instantaneous flow rate is exported and matches expected values.
